title: How do I get Item Details to show up on right side of window?
excerpt: I’ve been watching countless tutorial videos, and I notice when clicking the first column of a board opens the item details on vertical scroll format window on the right side of the screen.  Currently when I click an ite…

posts.author: AnderTran (Mike Anderson)
posts.cookedHtml:
I’ve been watching countless tutorial videos, and I notice when clicking the first column of a board opens the item details on vertical scroll format window on the right side of the screen. Currently when I click an item, it opens a new window inside the desktop to view full details.

I was wondering why my app is behaving differently, or how I can toggle between the full window popup, and the side screen format. I hope that I am explaining this clearly.

Thank you in advance for any advice.

Mike

posts.author: Clover_Gal (Desiree McCarthy | monday.com Ambassador)
posts.cookedHtml: It’s just a view within the item that you can set up. Go in, click the plus and then set it up how you’d like. I’d recommend starting with the Information section.

posts.author: Ag47Panda (Steve Wasson)
posts.cookedHtml:
@AnderTran I think you are talking about the item card view. This is standard for any item. Just hover on the end of the item name field name where it says “Open” and you can open these for all of them. You can also customize what is in the item card view as well. Good luck!

Screenshot 2025-01-27 at 11.04.55 AM1238×290 20 KB

posts.author: AnderTran (Mike Anderson)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello Steve!

I am referring to this view - the split screen, which shows item details on the right, while still showing the master list on the left.

Insights- Add Item Description Widget1943×1115 224 KB

Right now, when I open the item by clicking this:

Image 11920×1188 109 KB

A window opens up that looks like this:

Image 22541×1583 297 KB

It opens a full window covering everything on the entire workspace. In order to get back to the master list, I have to close this window.

I hope that better demonstrates my issue.

Thanks so much for your help on this,

Mike

posts.author: Ag47Panda (Steve Wasson)
posts.cookedHtml: Now I see it. It’s a difference between CRM and the Work Management products. I don’t use CRM so I am unfamiliar. I have requested backup from my fellow ambassadors.

posts.author: AnderTran (Mike Anderson)
posts.cookedHtml:
Thank you so much Steve -

I would love to figure out how to achieve this view.

Mike

posts.author: Clover_Gal (Desiree McCarthy | monday.com Ambassador)
posts.cookedHtml: Thanks Steve for clarifying that. @AnderTran I have both products and don’t know of a way to make the CRM ‘pop out’ smaller. Can I ask what your need to have the main screen still visible is from? ie do you need data from there?

posts.author: AnderTran (Mike Anderson)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello Desiree!

Since there is so much information in the row in which you need to scroll left to right, I just thought it would be cool to see a dedicated side window which would offer a vertical scroll format getting a birds eye view of those additional fields.

I could then just work my way down the master list on the left, and tap on each record to view that additional information on the right side. It just seems so much more efficient.

If I am understanding this correctly, Monday.com has different “modes” that it can work in? And since I am using it for CRM, this is why I am not seeing this functionality? Could you please elaborate on that more?

Thanks again for all your help,

Mike

posts.author: Clover_Gal (Desiree McCarthy | monday.com Ambassador)
posts.cookedHtml:
That helps me understand, thanks! What I’d recommend is just creating an item view inside that has the info from ALL of the columns across. Then you can use the arrows at the top right to switch between rows.

It’s not ‘modes’, it’s the ‘Product’ you’re using. You have the CRM product and that is why your pop out looks different. Hope that helps!

posts.author: WLane (Wally Lane)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @AnderTran you can achieve something similar by creating a new blank view and adding in the table widget. There are some considerations when you do this though, as the board widget has some limitations like you can’t sort.

image1852×814 218 KB

posts.author: AlichiaS (Ali Sawitoski)
posts.cookedHtml:
For us (Enterprise) this had to be enabled by our Account Rep. One thing to note about this view is that it removes [***] menu which allows users to manage notifications on a given item - really frustrating!

edit to add: the specific language in my email exchange from the rep was "feature flag I can flip on the back end to enable this view. However, it will change the behavior for everyone. " <

posts.author: MaxKP (Max)
posts.cookedHtml:
Great to know.

Monday - to whom do I have to reach out to if I want this enabled? (pro, not enterprise customer)?

posts.cookedHtml:
Is there an update on this?

Having items visible while reviewing updates in CRM is useful. Blocking the screen with empty space doesnt seem productive.
